summary: Added a project-local Control Runway bitmap texture and wired it into the first-screen runway, Visual Asset Vault, README, and regression tests.

implementation.style: dark premium game UI runway with cyan route rail, amber unlock sockets, coral blocker pips, glass panels, and subtle grid layers
consumers: Visual Asset Vault system asset registry

browser.finding: Computed Control Runway background includes control-runway-20260618.png, runway rendered 6 nodes with 1 active node, 3 visible Control sections, 0 document/body overflow, and no console warnings/errors.
Library deck Asset Vault includes the Control Runway registry entry and runway badge with 0 horizontal overflow and no console warnings/errors.

follow_up: Use the same texture quality standard for Worlds, History, and Library deck-specific compact surfaces.
Add canvas or sprite motion over the Control Runway texture if the next iteration focuses on richer movement.
Continue keeping generated assets local to the standalone repo so other agent-company forks can use them.
